title: wibta if i refuse to move back in with my current roommate?
text: ok. so. (on a throwaway) i have a roommate, let’s call them f. f is moving back in with their mom, so i’m moving into a new place. i’m currently taking a break from post-secondary, but i’m going back next fall. f also wants to start going to post-secondary, though the window has passed for them to start the same year i go back. however, when they do inevitably start post-secondary, they want to move back in with me because they, quote, “can’t live on their own”. when they first suggested this, i was fine with it, and so i agreed. besides, it’s over a year away— it’ll be fine, right? not so much. ever since we’ve been living together, due to their mental illnesses, i’ve basically been their caretaker, and over the past few months it’s been getting so much worse so fast. they will sometimes have episodes so bad that they can’t feed themselves, take their medication, borderline can’t even dress themselves. i have been in multiple situations where i’ve had to consider whether or not i needed to physically restrain them so they didn’t hurt me or themselves. i know i have no obligation to do this for them, but if i don’t then no one else will and then they will go hungry and unmedicated. they literally cannot take care of themselves.  so therein lies the crux of my problem: i cannot do this anymore. i cannot be their caretaker anymore. i don’t want to and i just can’t. i’m so unbelievably happy that they’re moving back into their mom’s house. i know i shouldn’t feel this way, it’s not their fault that they’re like this, but it’s so exhausting for me to deal with day in and day out. i know that if it feels this way for me it must be exponentially more for them, but i just. don’t really care anymore. i’m numbs to it from having to turn my emotions off all the time, so i can help them. it’s just exhausting. tl;dr: my roommate cannot take care of themselves, and so i feel obligated to while we’re living together. wibta if i refuse to move back in with them when they start post-secondary so i don’t have to be their quote-unquote caretaker?

storyboard.content: I have a roommate, F, who is moving back in with their mom. I'm moving into a new place and going back to post-secondary next fall.
F wants to move back in with me when they start post-secondary because they say they "can't live on their own." This would be over a year from now.
When F first suggested moving back in together, I agreed to it.
Due to F's mental illnesses, I have basically been their caretaker ever since we've been living together. Over the past few months it has been getting much worse very fast.

F sometimes has episodes so bad they can't feed themselves, take their medication, or borderline even dress themselves. I have been in situations where I had to consider physically restraining them so they didn't hurt me or themselves.
I know I have no obligation to do this for them, but if I don't, no one else will and they will go hungry and unmedicated.
I cannot be their caretaker anymore. It is so exhausting to deal with day in and day out, and I've become numb from having to turn my emotions off to help them.
I am considering refusing to move back in with F when they start post-secondary, going back on my earlier agreement.
